prompt: Publication-grade conceptual diagram for an academic website, landscape orientation, clean light background, flat 2D editorial institutional style. Central rounded rectangle labeled "UD CIS Dependable Agentic Systems Center" in dark blue "#00539f", centered horizontally and vertically in the composition. Three aligned internal modules inside or immediately below the center block labeled "Infrastructure", "Evaluation", and "Pilots". Four surrounding partner groups feeding into the center: upper left "Industry Partners", upper right "National Labs", lower left "Campus Partners", lower right "Education & Talent". Draw arrows from all four partner groups into the center. On the far right add an outcomes column with three stacked boxes labeled "Research Publications", "Reusable Platforms", and "Sponsored Projects" with arrows from the center toward that column. Use Delaware blue "#00539f" for the center block, gold accent "#FFD200" for the outcomes boxes, light grey dashed borders for partner groups, large readable labels, balanced whitespace, crisp connectors. No title, no caption, no 3D, no icons, no people, no mascots, no legend.

coreMessages: The Dependable Agentic Systems Center is the hub that transforms partner inputs into research outcomes.
Four distinct partner categories feed resources and collaboration into the center.
The center produces three categories of tangible outputs: publications, platforms, and sponsored projects.
Internal capabilities span infrastructure, evaluation, and pilot programs.

review.summary: The diagram successfully communicates all four core messages: the central hub role of the UD CIS Dependable Agentic Systems Center, the four partner categories feeding into it (Industry Partners, National Labs, Campus Partners, Education & Talent), the three output categories on the right (Research Publications, Reusable Platforms, Sponsored Projects), and the internal capabilities (Infrastructure, Evaluation, Pilots). Layout is clean with balanced whitespace and crisp connectors. The color scheme uses dark blue for the center block and gold/yellow accents for outcome boxes as requested. Partner groups have dashed borders as styling detail (which was in the omit list but is acceptable as it doesn't violate correctness). Text is legible at appropriate sizes. The house style is reasonably followed with the editorial institutional feel, though the blue (#00539f Delaware blue) deviates slightly from the house primaryStructure (#2C5282) — this is acceptable as the original request specifically called for Delaware blue. Arrow connectors are clear and show directional flow properly. No title, caption, 3D effects, icons, people, or legends are present as required. The figure meets the presentation publication threshold of 6.5/10.
